# UC-14.2.2 · Process Variable Anomalies

## Description

Process variables (pressure, flow, temperature) outside normal ranges indicate equipment failure or process upset. Early detection prevents safety incidents.

## Value

Process variables (pressure, flow, temperature) outside normal ranges indicate equipment failure or process upset. Early detection prevents safety incidents.

## Implementation

Ingest process variables via OPC-UA. Define normal ranges per tag. Use Edge Hub kNN anomaly detection for ML-based alerting. Alert on limit exceedances. Track process stability over time.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OPC-UA input, Edge Hub anomaly detection.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: OPC-UA/Modbus process data (analog values).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest process variables via OPC-UA. Define normal ranges per tag. Use Edge Hub kNN anomaly detection for ML-based alerting. Alert on limit exceedances. Track process stability over time.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="opcua:process"
| where value > high_limit OR value < low_limit
| table _time, tag_name, value, low_limit, high_limit, unit
```

Understanding this SPL

**Process Variable Anomalies** — Process variables (pressure, flow, temperature) outside normal ranges indicate equipment failure or process upset. Early detection prevents safety incidents.

Documented **Data sources**: OPC-UA/Modbus process data (analog values). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OPC-UA input, Edge Hub anomaly detection. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: opcua:process. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="opcua:process". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where value > high_limit OR value < low_limit` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Process Variable Anomalies**): table _time, tag_name, value, low_limit, high_limit, unit


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (process variable with limit bands), Table (out-of-range events), Single value (current value with status color).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="opcua:process"
| where value > high_limit OR value < low_limit
| table _time, tag_name, value, low_limit, high_limit, unit
```

## Visualization

Line chart (process variable with limit bands), Table (out-of-range events), Single value (current value with status color).
